prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: I think one under-discussed writer today is Kevin MIT, who writes at Tech Tab - the last ten U.S. presidents, and what they achieved (or tried to achieve) in their first 100 days. Everyone seems to focus on the topics of how a president precovers himself or gets forced into a war in his first hundred days. I believe that Kevin MIT's work is incredibly insightful and important. He shows how a president in his first hundred days lays out his vision and what he's trying to accomplish when he has the full support of the people who just elected him. His blog posts are quite substantial, and Kevin seems to always be on top of what he needs to communicate in a brilliant yet efficient manner.
